message:
fix(a11y): bound screen reader mirror announcements

bd-2vr05.7.3

files.patch:
@@ -1215,7 +1215,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.7","title":"feature(frankenterm-js): accessibility, focus model, and IME composition robustness","description":"Background
- Accessibility and IME are required for inclusive and global terminal usage.
- Canvas/WebGPU terminals can easily regress if these are bolted on late.

Goal
- Make accessibility and IME first-class, deterministic behaviors in FrankenTermJS.

In Scope
- Composition pipeline for IME preedit/commit flows.
- Focus model and keyboard-only navigation guarantees.
- Screen-reader mirror and live announcement policy.
- Reduced-motion and high-contrast integration.
- Cross-browser E2E accessibility matrix.

Out of Scope
- Minimal checkbox-only a11y claims without test evidence.

Why This Matters
- Necessary for real-world adoption and accessibility obligations.

Acceptance Criteria
- IME and keyboard-only paths function reliably.
- Screen-reader mirror behavior is coherent and bounded.
- Browser matrix tests provide reproducible evidence.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Feature behavior is validated by comprehensive unit tests that cover core invariants and edge cases.
- Feature behavior is validated by end-to-end test scripts that exercise realistic user workflows.
- E2E runs emit detailed structured logs (JSONL or equivalent) sufficient for deterministic failure triage and replay.
- CI gates for this feature fail on regression in correctness, determinism, or declared standards scope.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:36.160403481Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:15.419218131Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["accessibility","frankenterm","ime","input"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.7.1","title":"task(input): implement IME composition pipeline with deterministic preedit handling","description":"Preedit/commit/cancel handling that avoids dropped or reordered composed input.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Behavior in this task is deterministic for fixed inputs and capability profile.
- Required unit and/or E2E tests are added for the scoped behavior.
- Scope is complete only when dependencies remain satisfied and no regressions are introduced.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- This task must add or update focused unit tests for core logic touched.
- This task must link to at least one end-to-end script path that validates integrated behavior.
- Validation output must include structured logs detailed enough for deterministic postmortem analysis.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- IME preedit/commit paths are reliable across tested input methods.
- Focus and keyboard-only workflows are deterministic.
- Screen-reader mirror and announcements are bounded and coherent.
- Accessibility browser matrix tests pass with reproducible logs.

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","notes":"Execution Notes
- Accessibility and IME are first-class quality targets, not deferred polish.
- Focus model must be robust in embedded app contexts with nested focus scopes.
- Live announcement behavior should stay bounded to avoid noisy assistive output.
- Browser matrix tests should include reproducible logs and deterministic replay hooks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MossyDeer","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:48.039543818Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-19T16:37:17.854003390Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T16:37:17.853983643Z","close_reason":"Implemented deterministic IME event pipeline across core/web/widgets/runtime with focused tests and rch validation gates.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenterm","ime","input"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2vr05.8","title":"feature(frankenterm-js): renderer hardening, fallback backends, and perf leadership","description":"Background
- WebGPU-first performance is a project pillar, but deployment reality requires graceful fallback where WebGPU is unavailable.
- Renderer regressions can silently degrade UX without explicit parity/perf gates.

Goal
- Harden rendering stack: keep WebGPU leadership, add fallback capability, and enforce measurable performance constraints.

In Scope
- Backend abstraction preserving one patch contract.
- Canvas2D fallback path.
- WebGPU patch-upload and atlas optimization.
- Backend parity validation via deterministic frame comparison.
- Comparative benchmarks versus xterm.js workloads.
- Memory/frame budget guardrails.

Out of Scope
- Fallback behavior that drifts semantically from primary backend.

Why This Matters
- Ensures FrankenTermJS is both fast and practical across environments.

Acceptance Criteria
- WebGPU path meets performance targets.
- Fallback remains correct and test-verified.
- Budget guards prevent silent runaway degradation.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Feature behavior is validated by comprehensive unit tests that cover core invariants and edge cases.
- Feature behavior is validated by end-to-end test scripts that exercise realistic user workflows.
- E2E runs emit detailed structured logs (JSONL or equivalent) sufficient for deterministic failure triage and replay.
- CI gates for this feature fail on regression in correctness, determinism, or declared standards scope.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:36.609282874Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-16T17:05:52.150540345Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T17:05:52.150521079Z","close_reason":"Completed: renderer hardening feature child tasks bd-2vr05.8.1 through bd-2vr05.8.6 are all closed (backend abstraction, Canvas2D fallback, WebGPU fast paths, parity suite, perf harness, budget guardrails)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenterm","performance","renderer","webgpu"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -11,6 +11,7 @@ set -euo pipefail
 SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
 PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
 LIB_DIR="$PROJECT_ROOT/tests/e2e/lib"
+PRESET_E2E_JSONL_FILE="${E2E_JSONL_FILE:-}"
 
 # shellcheck source=/dev/null
 if [[ -f "$LIB_DIR/common.sh" ]]; then
@@ -50,13 +51,15 @@ TIMESTAMP="$(e2e_log_stamp)"
 LOG_DIR="${LOG_DIR:-/tmp/ftui-a11y-transitions-${E2E_RUN_ID}-${TIMESTAMP}}"
 E2E_LOG_DIR="$LOG_DIR"
 E2E_RESULTS_DIR="${E2E_RESULTS_DIR:-$LOG_DIR/results}"
-E2E_JSONL_FILE="${E2E_JSONL_FILE:-$LOG_DIR/a11y_transitions_e2e.jsonl}"
+E2E_JSONL_FILE="${A11Y_TRANSITIONS_JSONL_FILE:-${PRESET_E2E_JSONL_FILE:-$LOG_DIR/a11y_transitions_e2e.jsonl}}"
+E2E_CHILD_JSONL_DIR="${E2E_CHILD_JSONL_DIR:-$LOG_DIR/child-jsonl}"
 E2E_RUN_CMD="${E2E_RUN_CMD:-$0 $*}"
 E2E_RUN_START_MS="${E2E_RUN_START_MS:-$(e2e_run_start_ms)}"
 export E2E_LOG_DIR E2E_RESULTS_DIR E2E_JSONL_FILE E2E_RUN_CMD E2E_RUN_START_MS
-mkdir -p "$E2E_LOG_DIR" "$E2E_RESULTS_DIR"
+mkdir -p "$E2E_LOG_DIR" "$E2E_RESULTS_DIR" "$E2E_CHILD_JSONL_DIR"
 jsonl_init
 jsonl_assert "artifact_log_dir" "pass" "log_dir=$E2E_LOG_DIR"
+jsonl_assert "child_jsonl_dir" "pass" "child_jsonl_dir=$E2E_CHILD_JSONL_DIR"
 jsonl_set_context "host" "${COLUMNS:-}" "${LINES:-}" "${E2E_SEED:-0}"
 
 PASSED=0
@@ -125,10 +128,20 @@ fi
 
 run_step "a11y_transition_tests" bash -c "
     cd '$PROJECT_ROOT' &&
-    E2E_JSONL=1 A11Y_TEST_SEED=\${A11Y_TEST_SEED:-0} \
+    E2E_JSONL=1 \
+    E2E_JSONL_FILE='$E2E_CHILD_JSONL_DIR/a11y_transition_tests.jsonl' \
+    A11Y_TEST_SEED=\${A11Y_TEST_SEED:-0} \
         cargo test -p ftui-demo-showcase --test a11y_snapshots -- a11y_transition --nocapture
 "
 
+run_step "screen_reader_mirror_policy_tests" bash -c "
+    cd '$PROJECT_ROOT' &&
+    E2E_JSONL=1 \
+    E2E_JSONL_FILE='$E2E_CHILD_JSONL_DIR/screen_reader_mirror_policy_tests.jsonl' \
+    A11Y_TEST_SEED=\${A11Y_TEST_SEED:-0} \
+        cargo test -p ftui-a11y --test a11y_tests -- screen_reader --nocapture
+"
+
 echo ""
 echo "=========================================="
 TOTAL=$((PASSED + FAILED + SKIPPED))
